subject: Physics
overall_learning_objectives: Explain the laws of reflection with proper ray diagrams
Analyze image formation in different types of mirrors
Differentiate between concave and convex mirrors based on properties and applications
Understand the structure and working of convex and concave lenses
Investigate how object distance affects image formation
Apply concepts of reflection and lenses to real-life optical devices

lesson_plan.title: Nature of Light and Reflection
topics_covered: Incident ray, reflected ray and normal
student_activities: Observe reflection using a mirror and light source
Draw ray diagrams showing incident and reflected rays

lesson_plan.title: Laws of Reflection and Ray Diagrams
student_activities: Measure angles of incidence and reflection in diagrams

lesson_plan.title: Image Formation in Plane Mirrors
student_activities: Observe mirror images and record characteristics

lesson_plan.title: Concave and Convex Mirrors
topics_covered: Applications of concave and convex mirrors
student_activities: Identify mirrors used in headlights, shaving mirrors and rear-view mirrors
Compare properties of concave and convex mirrors

lesson_plan.title: Optical Devices and Chapter Synthesis
student_activities: Identify mirrors or lenses used in everyday devices

teacher_tip: Emphasize ray diagrams and real-life applications of mirrors and lenses so students develop analytical understanding rather than memorizing definitions.
